title: Fleischmannia magdalenensis
taxonomy: Fleischmannia magdalenensis is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants (angiosperms) in the clade Rosidae. Further, it is classified within the tribe Eupatorieae, a group of plants known for their unique inflorescences. The genus Fleischmannia is a member of the subfamily Mutisioideae. The classification of Fleischmannia magdalenensis also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Fleischmannia magdalenensis is a plant species commonly known as "Magdalena Fleischmannia" or "Palo de la Magdalena", belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Fleischmannia magdalenensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Fleischmannia magdalenensis is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, colds, and digestive issues.
